3 bullets.

prompts.label: Query depth and complexity limits
prompts.text:
Explain how deeply nested queries can overload resolvers.
Describe depth limits and complexity scoring conceptually.
Mention persisted operations as optional advanced mitigation.
Discuss introspection settings carefully without dogma.
Encourage observability on slow fields.

prompts.label: DataLoader batching pattern
prompts.text:
Introduce batching per-request loads to collapse many resolver calls into fewer DB queries.
Explain ordering nondeterminism pitfalls lightly.
Compare to doing manual joins in some cases honestly.
Advise measuring hot paths.
Avoid treating GraphQL like magic SQL.

prompts.label: Schema ownership monolith versus federated
prompts.text:
Contrast one graph owned by one team versus federated subgraphs culturally.
Advise beginners mastering one service graph first realistically.
Mention versioning and deprecation practices carry over.
Warn about operational overhead early.
Encourage humane cross-team coordination.

prompts.label: Errors as data partial results
prompts.text:
Explain returning partial successes with typed errors thoughtfully.
Mention unions versus nullable patterns at a beginner level.
Advise humane client-visible messages.
Encourage documenting error shapes.
Relate observability tying errors to traces lightly.

prompts.label: Persisted queries allowlists
prompts.text:
Describe pinning known operations acceptable in prod especially mobile apps.
Mention build-time operation registration pipelines lightly.
Warn about disabling authz server-side mistakenly.
Advise versioning operations.
Relate CDN caching pitfalls historically.

prompts.label: Schema explorers for onboarding
prompts.text:
Encourage using graph explorers or generated docs as learning scaffolding.
Mention introspection policy decisions vary by exposure.
Advise pairing exploration with glossary domain terms.
Point toward codegen tooling as next step moderately.
Caution dumping huge SDL on newcomers.

prompts.label: Subscriptions versus polling realistically
prompts.text:
Differentiate websocket subscriptions overhead versus pragmatic polling starter.
Mention infra scaling subscriptions cautiously briefly.
Advise solid mutations and queries mastery first.
Mention SSE as middle ground casually.
Encourage budgeting backend time honestly.

prompts.label: Custom scalars for clarity
prompts.text:
Advocate custom scalars to reduce ambiguous strings moderately.
Mention coercion rules needing documentation.
Relate codegen mapping ergonomics mildly.
Advise timezone clarity for datetime types.
Keep migration stories gentle.

prompts.label: Resolver context authorization
prompts.text:
Explain injecting authenticated viewer identity into resolver context cleanly.
Mention directives like auth rules as optional advanced ergonomics.
Emphasize field-level authorization still server-side enforced.
Advise integration tests forbidding forbidden fields.
Encourage auditing suspicious query patterns.

prompts.label: Schema compatibility checks
prompts.text:
Describe CI checks failing when removing fields without deprecation process.
Mention tools comparing schemas lightly optionally.
Advise communicating sunset timelines politely.
Relate federation contract testing pointer lightly.
Promote empathy for API consumers.
